title: ajuga oocephala
intro: Ajuga oocephala, also known as Bugle, is a perennial plant species in the mint family, native to the Himalayas and parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Ajuga oocephala include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and exhibit antioxidant effects that may help protect against cell damage. The herbal preparations of Ajuga oocephala typically involve its leaves being dried and made into teas, infusions, or used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine formulations.
